Doc 1 Platonium (atomic symbol: Pt, atomic weight: 195.09), is a recently discovered synthetic platinum isotope that was discovered to be the cause of extensive radioactivity problems with the metal. Platonium was prepared by irradiating natural platinum with high energy neutrons, such as occur in a thermal nuclear reactor. It was first naturally produced as a contaminant in a spot of eight-nannomole worth of extracted platinum, with a 58 percent specific activity, in the Lincoln Near-Earth Asteroid Research (LINEAR) program's ongoing astronomical survey for near-Earth objects.

In effect, mortality risk for a given temperature is higher at both the warm extreme (<13°C) and the cold extreme (<=-1°C), and there is a U-shaped relationship between mortality risk and temperature between these bounds. Annual mortality risk shows a U-shaped association with temperature that is shallower prior to year-end. Moreover, as the season progresses, individuals who are frail or elderly have mortality risk that is even more sensitive to temperature and that is still elevated by stratospheric temperatures. Thus, cold-related mortality is more pronounced and may persist well into winter.
In humans, this preference for the cold is evident in the very common practice of covering up with additional clothing during the early stages of shivering. Although animals may have an adaptative advantage with respect to shivering, enabling them to obtain heat from the expenditure of energy, in humans the near reflexive tendency to obtain more heat through clothing appears an adaptation to living in cold environments, designed to preserve metabolic energy for other activities.

".mil" is the second-level domain for the United States Department of Defense (DoD) Internet network, also known as the DOD Network (at least when used outside the DoD). In each of these cases, the top-level domain, or simply 1. domain, is the topmost (highest level) entity in the domain hierarchy. Essen Essen (; Latin: ""Assindia"") is the central and second largest city of the Ruhr, the largest urban area in Germany. <information>Doc 11 Top100 tallest self-standing tower structures By Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia A self-supporting skyscraper or tower is a tall building in which there is no external support — or with external support only on the lower section. This type of building is extremely rare, and few have been built, most being 20th century technologically complex structures with traditional architecture. The Tall Building Institute of America or Canada gives tower classification scale of 100 to 1 to designate the self-supporting height of buildings. For example, Empire State Building, a noted hill construction located in the heart of New York City has a self support height of: 99.84 (6 acres); while Burj Kahlid is 0.25 (5 acres). Self-supporting skyscrapers appear briefly in the art of ancient China, when Colonel Soong Chiao Singer, among others, co-constructed a building in 1963 with 12 floors. There are currently no known examples in the world. A self-supporting tower can be formed by sliding a free ionizing beam between the atom of a structural mass. Many self-supporting structures will have a tiny fracture which acts as a stabilizing point for the structure. Almost all of these crystal structures are formed by a single material, but since the 20th century silver, platinum, gold, platinum-iron and rhodium have also been used. It is well known in Sweden as the central site of the annual Almedalen Week. In medieval times the area, just outside the city walls, was the city harbour. Due to the post-glacial rebound, the port over time grew too shallow and a new one had to be constructed further away from the city, where the sea is deeper. Sevilla Tower The Sevilla Tower (), known until 2015 as the Cajasol Tower or Pelli Tower, is an office skyscraper in Seville, Spain. Its construction started in March 2008 and was completed in 2015. The tower is tall and has 40 floors. It is an office building, with the entrance to the tower located off Odiel street. The tower is the tallest building in Andalusia and in the city of Seville, and the seventh tallest in Spain. The 16-storey Norra Kungstornet is and was built between 1919 and 1924; and the taller 17-storey, Södra Kungstornet was built between 1924 and 1925. Together, they are considered the first modern skyscrapers in Europe. The world's tallest active lighthouse is the Godthåbsfjord lighthouse in Greenland. The Godthåbsfjord lighthouse was built between 1972 and 1974 and is 186 metres (610 ft) tall. The construction of the lighthouse began in 1972 and was completed in 1974. The ILS-UT 240 Radar, made by ILS, was installed in 1984 at the top of the tower. The lighthouse was officially opened on 17 August 1984 by Prime Minister Palle Juul.

"Cogito, ergo sum"
As a critic of Cartesian subjectivity, Heidegger sought to ground human subjectivity in death as that certainty which individualizes and authenticates our being. As he wrote in 1927: ""This certainty, that ""I myself am in that I will die,"" is the basic certainty of Dasein itself. It is a genuine statement of Dasein, while ""cogito sum"" is only the semblance of such a statement. If such pointed formulations mean anything at all, then the appropriate statement pertaining to Dasein in its being would have to be ""sum moribundus"" [I am in dying], ""moribundus"" not as someone gravely ill or wounded,

"Mr. Cogito"
Mr. Cogito Mr. Cogito Mr. Cogito is a character created by Zbigniew Herbert, a Polish poet and essayist. He first appears in a poem entitled ""The Envoy of Mr. Cogito"" (""Przesłanie Pana Cogito"") published in 1973. Initially Mr. Cogito was an Everyman, a universal element of humanity sharing his opinions on various aspects of life and existence. However, the more he says, the more disembodied he appears, and becomes transformed into an ethical symbol and a metaphor of the tough choices we have to make between good and evil. The character's name originates from Descartes' famous phrase, ""Cogito ergo sum.""

It emphasizes the value of individual action, global unity, and the role of faith and spirituality in promoting human dignity, justice, equality, and understanding.
